NVDA.summary: NVIDIA reported Q2 2026 EPS of $1.87, beating estimates by 5.5%, driven by record Data Center revenue of $75.2 billion, up 92% year-over-year, and strong growth in Edge Computing. The company's performance was driven by the buildout of AI factories, with management citing the adoption of agentic AI and the company's position at the center of this transformation. The stock reacted positively, moving up 6.3% on earnings day, driven by the strong headline beat and record revenue, as well as the company's increased quarterly cash dividend and additional $80 billion share repurchase authorization.
NVDA.keyQuote: The buildout of AI factories — the largest infrastructure expansion in human history — is accelerating at extraordinary speed.

NVDA.summary: NVIDIA reported Q1 2026 EPS of $1.62, beating estimates by 5.3%, driven by record quarterly Data Center revenue of $62.3 billion, up 22% from Q3 and 75% from a year ago. The strong performance was driven by the major platform shifts in accelerated computing and AI, with management citing the success of the NVIDIA Blackwell and Rubin platforms, as well as partnerships with major cloud providers. The stock reacted positively, moving up 7.9% on earnings day, driven by the headline beat and strong forward guidance, including revenue expectations of $78.0 billion for Q1 2027.
NVDA.keyQuote: Computing demand is growing exponentially — the agentic AI inflection point has arrived. Grace Blackwell with NVLink is the king of inference today — delivering an order-of-magnitude lower cost per token — and Vera Rubin will extend that leadership even further.

AMD.summary: AMD reported Q1 2026 EPS of $1.37, beating estimates by 5.8%, driven by strong performance in the data center segment. The company's data center AI accelerator opportunity and strategic partnerships, such as the one with Meta, were key drivers of the beat. The stock reacted positively, moving up 18.6% on earnings day, driven by the headline beat and the company's strong financial foundation.
AMD.keyQuote: AMD's large growth opportunities across diverse set of markets; AMD's data center AI accelerator opportunity; the expected plans, benefits and timing of AMD's strategic collaborations; the strategic partnership with Meta and the deployment of up to six gigawatts of AMD Instinct GPUs

AMD.summary: AMD reported Q4 2025 EPS of $1.53, beating estimates by 16.0%, driven by strong performance in the data center segment, although specific numbers are not provided in the given text. The company's ability to expand its data center and AI leadership, as well as its large growth opportunities across diverse markets, were key areas of focus for investors. The stock moved -17.3% on earnings day, suggesting that despite the EPS beat, forward guidance or segment performance may not have met investor expectations.

AVGO.summary: Broadcom reported Q2 EPS of $2.44, beating estimates by 1.7%, driven by a 143% year-over-year growth in AI semiconductor revenue to $10.8 billion, which exceeded forecasts. The strong performance was attributed to increasing demand for custom AI accelerators and AI networking, with management citing accelerating growth in AI semiconductor revenue. The stock fell 12.6% despite the beat, likely due to the forward guidance, which although robust, may not have met elevated investor expectations, particularly the third quarter revenue guidance of $29.4 billion, an 84% year-over-year increase.
AVGO.keyQuote: Q2 semiconductor revenue from AI of $10.8 billion grew 143% year-over-year, above our forecast, driven by increasing demand for custom AI accelerators and AI networking,

AVGO.summary: Broadcom reported Q1 EPS of $2.05, beating estimates by 1.3%, driven by a 106% year-over-year growth in AI semiconductor solutions revenue to $8.4 billion, which exceeded forecasts. The strong performance was attributed to robust demand for custom AI accelerators and AI networking, with management citing accelerating AI revenue growth. The stock reacted positively, moving up 7.2% on earnings day, driven by the headline beat and strong forward guidance, including a 47% year-over-year revenue growth expectation for Q2 to $22.0 billion.
AVGO.keyQuote: Our AI revenue growth is accelerating, and we expect AI semiconductor revenue to be $10.7 billion in Q2.

MRVL.summary: Marvell reported Q2 2026 EPS of $0.80, beating estimates by 0.6%, driven by strength in its data center business, with revenue growing 28% year-over-year to $2.418 billion. Management cited exceptional AI-related bookings and strong demand for specific products, including 800G and 1.6T scale-out optics, which matched investor focus on these areas. The stock reacted positively, moving up 32.5%, driven by the raised revenue outlook for fiscal 2027 and 2028, as well as the strong guidance for the second quarter, with expected revenue of $2.700 billion.
MRVL.keyQuote: We are seeing exceptional AI-related bookings, and as a result, we are significantly raising Marvell's revenue outlook for both fiscal 2027 and fiscal 2028 compared with the guidance we provided last quarter.

MRVL.summary: Marvell reported Q4 EPS of $0.80, beating estimates by 1.1%, driven by robust AI demand and record revenue of $2.219 billion, with the data center business being a key growth driver. The company's strong performance was attributed to continued strength in AI demand, with management citing design wins in fiscal 2026 hitting an all-time record, which is expected to fuel future growth. The stock reacted positively, moving up 18.4% on earnings day, driven by the headline beat and forward guidance, which includes expected year-over-year revenue growth acceleration in fiscal 2027.
MRVL.keyQuote: We expect year-over-year revenue growth to accelerate each quarter in fiscal 2027, driven by continued strength in our data center business, with bookings continuing to grow at a record pace.

TSM.summary: TSMC reported Q1 2026 EPS of $3.49, beating estimates by 4.7%, driven by strong demand from various segments, with revenue reaching NT$1,134.10 billion. The company's performance was driven by its advanced technology capacity and fab construction, with management citing long-term capacity plans based on market demand forecasts and TSMC's technology development roadmap. The stock moved +6.4% on earnings day, likely due to the headline beat and the approval of capital appropriations for advanced technology capacity and fab construction, which suggests a positive outlook for the company's future growth.
TSM.keyQuote: To meet long-term capacity plans based on market demand forecasts and TSMC's technology development roadmap, the Board approved capital appropriations of approximately US$31,284.30 million for purposes mainly including: 1) Installation of advanced technology capacity; 2) Fab construction, and installation of fab facility systems.

TSM.summary: TSMC reported Q4 2025 EPS of $3.14, beating estimates by 5.5%, driven by strong demand from various segments, although specific segment details were not provided. The company's performance was likely driven by its advanced technology capacity and packaging services, which have been in high demand. The stock reacted positively, moving up 5.5% on the earnings day, likely due to the headline beat and the company's capital appropriation plans for future growth.

ASML.summary: ASML reported Q1 2026 EPS of $7.15, beating estimates by 8.0%, driven by strong demand for its lithography systems, particularly in the semiconductor industry, with total net sales of €8.8 billion. The company's performance was driven by ongoing AI-related infrastructure investments, which led to increased demand for chips and accelerated capacity expansion plans by its customers. The stock reacted positively, moving up 8.8% on earnings day, largely due to the company's raised 2026 total net sales guidance to €36-40 billion, with a gross margin of 51-53%.
ASML.keyQuote: The semiconductor industry's growth outlook continues to solidify, driven by ongoing AI-related infrastructure investments. Demand for chips is outpacing supply. In response, our customers are accelerating their capacity expansion plans for 2026 and beyond, supported by long-term agreements with their customers.

ASML.summary: ASML reported Q4 EPS of $7.34, missing estimates by 2.7%, with total net sales of €9.7 billion driven by record EUV sales and growth in installed base business sales. Management cited a notable step-up in customers' medium-term capacity plans, driven by robust expectations of AI-related demand, as a key driver of performance. The stock moved up 8.8% on the earnings day, likely driven by the company's forward guidance of 2026 total net sales between €34 billion and €39 billion, which suggests a growth year ahead.
ASML.keyQuote: In the last months, many of our customers have shared a notably more positive assessment of the medium-term market situation, primarily based on more robust expectations of the sustainability of AI-related demand.

ARM.summary: Arm Holdings reported Q4 2025 EPS of $0.43, beating estimates by 5.1%, driven by strong performance in license and royalty revenue segments, with specific numbers not provided in the given text. The company's performance was likely driven by growth in key product lines and customer demand, although specific details are not available. The stock reacted positively, moving up 11.6% on the earnings day, likely due to the headline beat and potential positive forward guidance, although the exact drivers are unclear.

MU.summary: Micron reported a significant earnings beat with EPS of $12.20, exceeding estimates by 33.2%, driven by strong demand in its Cloud Memory and Core Data Center business units, which reported revenues of $7,749 million and $5,687 million, respectively. The company's performance was driven by a strong demand environment, tight industry supply, and its strategic position in the AI era, with management citing memory as a strategic asset for its customers. The stock reacted negatively, falling 9.9% on earnings day, likely due to the forward guidance, which although strong, may not have met investor expectations, with revenue guidance of $33.5 billion and gross margin of approximately 81% for the third quarter.
MU.keyQuote: In the AI era, memory has become a strategic asset for our customers, and we are investing in our global manufacturing footprint to support their growing demand.

MU.summary: Micron reported Q1 EPS of $4.78, beating estimates by 20.6%, driven by strong performance in its Cloud Memory Business Unit, which saw revenue of $5.284 billion and gross margin of 66%. The company's performance was driven by accelerating AI demand and strong execution across its business units, with management citing its technology leadership and differentiated product portfolio as key factors. The stock reacted positively to the earnings beat and strong forward guidance, with the company forecasting Q2 revenue of $18.70 billion and non-GAAP EPS of $8.42, driving a 10.5% increase in the stock price on earnings day.
MU.keyQuote: Our Q2 outlook reflects substantial records across revenue, gross margin, EPS and free cash flow, and we anticipate our business performance to continue strengthening through fiscal 2026.

INTC.summary: Intel reported a significant earnings beat with EPS of $0.29, surpassing estimates by 2108.7%, driven by a 22% year-over-year increase in Data Center and AI (DCAI) revenue, which reached $5.1 billion. The strong performance was attributed to growing demand for Intel's CPUs and wafer and advanced packaging offerings, particularly in the AI era, with management citing the launch of new products such as Intel Xeon 600 processors and collaborations with Google and NVIDIA. The stock reacted positively, moving up 23.6% on earnings day, largely due to the headline beat and robust revenue growth, as well as the company's disciplined execution and expanded available supply.
INTC.keyQuote: The next wave of AI will bring intelligence closer to the end user, moving from foundational models to inference to agentic. This shift is significantly increasing the need for Intel’s CPUs and wafer and advanced packaging offerings.

QCOM.summary: Qualcomm reported Q1 2026 EPS of $2.65, beating estimates by 3.6%, driven by a 20% year-over-year growth in combined QCT Automotive and IoT revenues, which reached $2.7 billion. The company's performance was driven by strong demand in the automotive and IoT segments, with QCT Automotive revenues growing 38% year-over-year to $1.326 billion. The stock reacted positively, moving up 15.1% on earnings day, driven by the headline beat and the company's announcement of a new $20 billion share repurchase authorization.
QCOM.keyQuote: We are in a period of profound industry transformation — the rise of AI agents is reshaping our roadmap across every platform we develop.

QCOM.summary: Qualcomm reported Q1 FY26 EPS of $3.50, beating estimates by 2.9%, driven by record QCT revenues, particularly in the automotive segment, which reached $1.1 billion, up 15% year-over-year. Management cited strong demand for premium and high-tier smartphones, despite industry-wide memory supply constraints, which impacted near-term handset outlook. The stock fell 8.5% on earnings day, likely due to forward guidance that included the estimated impact of memory supply constraints on demand from several handset customers, with Q2 FY26 revenue guidance of $10.2-11.0 billion, below some expectations.
QCOM.keyQuote: While our near-term handsets outlook is impacted by industry-wide memory supply constraints, we are encouraged by end-consumer demand for premium and high tier smartphones, and remain on track to achieve our fiscal 2029 revenue goals.

AMAT.summary: Applied Materials reported Q2 EPS of $2.86, beating estimates by 6.5%, driven by record revenue of $7.91 billion, up 11% year over year, with the Semiconductor Systems segment being a key contributor, accounting for 75% of total revenue. The company's performance was driven by the rapid global build-out of AI computing infrastructure, with management citing strong leadership positions in leading-edge logic, DRAM, and advanced packaging. The stock reacted positively, moving up 11.2% on the earnings day, likely due to the strong headline beat and the company's raised guidance for the third quarter, with expected revenue of $8.95 billion and non-GAAP diluted EPS of $3.36.
AMAT.keyQuote: The rapid global build-out of AI computing infrastructure combined with Applied's strong leadership positions in leading-edge logic, DRAM and advanced packaging provide an exceptionally strong foundation for sustained, multi-year revenue and profit growth.

AMAT.summary: Applied Materials reported Q1 EPS of $2.38, beating estimates by 7.9%, driven by record DRAM revenue in the Semiconductor Systems segment, which accounted for 34% of the segment's revenue. The company's performance was fueled by the acceleration of industry investments in AI computing, with management citing high growth rates for leading-edge logic, high-bandwidth memory, and advanced packaging. The stock fell 8.3% despite the beat, likely due to the revenue guidance for Q2, which was in line with expectations, and the lack of upside to estimates, as the non-GAAP diluted EPS guidance of $2.64 was only slightly above the current consensus.
AMAT.keyQuote: The need for higher performance and more energy-efficient chips is driving high growth rates for leading-edge logic, high-bandwidth memory and advanced packaging. These are areas where Applied is the process equipment leader, and we expect to grow our semiconductor equipment business over 20 percent this calendar year.

LRCX.summary: Lam Research reported Q4 2025 EPS of $1.27, beating estimates by 8.7%, driven by strong performance in its wafer fabrication equipment and services business, although specific segment details were not provided. The company's leadership transition, with Sesha Varadarajan becoming COO and Karthik Rammohan assuming an expanded role, is expected to drive operational velocity and growth in the AI era. The stock fell 8.8% despite the earnings beat, possibly due to concerns over the leadership transition or forward guidance, which was not explicitly provided in the release.
LRCX.keyQuote: With Sesha's and Karthik's newly expanded roles, we are increasing velocity across our operations from product strategy and manufacturing to installation and maintenance to drive Lam's outperformance in the AI era.

KLAC.summary: KLA reported Q1 2026 EPS of $0.94, beating estimates by 2.5%, driven by strong revenue of $3.415 billion, which exceeded the midpoint of the guidance range, with the company's process control and process-enabling solutions contributing to the beat. The performance was driven by the company's critical role in the AI ecosystem and the global AI infrastructure buildout, with management citing continued market share momentum in process control. The stock reacted positively, moving up 8.0% on the earnings day, driven by the headline beat and the company's increased dividend and additional $7 billion stock repurchase authorization, which underscored management's confidence in the company's durable value creation.
KLAC.keyQuote: KLA delivered strong March quarter results exceeding the midpoint of our guidance ranges on both revenue and earnings per share. Our business momentum remains robust, and we are highly confident in our outlook for calendar year 2026

KLAC.summary: KLA reported Q4 2025 EPS of $0.89, beating estimates by 0.6%, driven by a record quarter for revenue, non-GAAP operating income, and free cash flow generation, with the company's differentiated product portfolio and solid execution in the foundry/logic and memory markets contributing to the beat. Management cited the increasing relevance of process control at the leading edge for foundry/logic and memory as a key driver of performance, which was in line with investor expectations going into the quarter. The stock reacted negatively, falling 15.2% on earnings day, likely due to the forward guidance, which may not have been as strong as investors had hoped, despite the company's positive outlook on its position in the AI ecosystem and the buildout of AI infrastructure across various growth vectors.
KLAC.keyQuote: KLA delivered a record quarter and calendar 2025 for revenue, non-GAAP operating income, and free cash flow generation. This performance was fueled by our differentiated product portfolio and solid company execution in an environment where the relevance of process control at the leading edge for foundry/logic and memory is increasing.

SNPS.summary: Synopsys reported Q2 EPS of $3.35, beating estimates by 6.0%, driven by strong performance across the business, particularly in the Design Automation segment, with revenue of $2.276 billion exceeding prior guidance. Management cited AI-driven semiconductor demand, architectural diversity, and complexity of chips and systems as key drivers of growth, with CEO Sassine Ghazi noting the company's momentum, leadership roadmap, and deep customer engagements as a strong foundation for sustained growth. The stock fell 8.6% despite the beat, likely due to forward guidance that, while raised, may not have fully met investor expectations, particularly given the impending close of the Processor IP Solutions business and its impact on revenue.
SNPS.keyQuote: AI is scaling semiconductor demand, architectural diversity and complexity of chips and the systems they power—driving demand across our portfolio.

SNPS.summary: Synopsys reported Q1 EPS of $3.77, beating estimates by 6.0%, driven by strong performance in its Design Automation segment, which includes advanced silicon design and verification products. The company's CEO cited AI-fueled robust system-level and semiconductor R&D as a key driver of growth, with increasing AI capabilities throughout the portfolio strengthening the company's strategic advantage. The stock fell 8.5% despite the beat, likely due to forward guidance that was in line with expectations, rather than exceeding them, and potentially disappointing investors who were looking for a stronger outlook.
SNPS.keyQuote: AI continues to fuel robust system-level and semiconductor R&D, and the increasing AI capabilities throughout our portfolio strengthen our strategic advantage and accelerate our customers’ innovation.

CDNS.summary: Cadence reported Q1 2026 EPS of $1.96, beating estimates by 4.0%, driven by strong performance in its core EDA business, which grew 18% year-over-year, and record hardware demand from AI and high-performance computing customers. The company's AI-driven solutions and expanding customer adoption were key drivers of performance, with management citing accelerating AI demand and a record backlog of $8.0 billion. The stock reacted positively, moving up 8.5% on the earnings day, driven by the headline beat and raised 2026 revenue outlook to 17% year-over-year growth.
CDNS.keyQuote: Cadence had a strong start to 2026, delivering a solid Q1 with accelerating AI demand and record backlog, reflecting strong customer commitment to our AI-driven portfolio.

CDNS.summary: Cadence reported Q4 EPS of $1.99, beating estimates by 4.1%, driven by 14% revenue growth with strong performance in its Core EDA business, which grew 13% in 2025, and its IP business, which grew nearly 25% year-over-year. The company's performance was driven by broad hyperscaler adoption, expanding digital full-flow deployments, and growing proliferation of its AI-driven products, with management citing strong customer demand for its expanding AI-driven product portfolio. The stock reacted positively, moving up 7.6% on the earnings day, driven by the headline beat and the company's record backlog of $7.8 billion, as well as its forward guidance, which includes revenue growth and non-GAAP operating margin expansion.
CDNS.keyQuote: Cadence delivered excellent results for the fourth quarter, closing an outstanding 2025 with over 14% revenue growth and 20% non-GAAP EPS growth, Strong customer demand for our expanding AI-driven product portfolio and the essential nature of Cadence's engineering software position us well to capture the massive opportunities in the AI era.

GFS.summary: GlobalFoundries (GFS) reported Q1 2026 EPS of $0.40, beating estimates by 14.0%, driven by growth in AI-centric markets and its unique global manufacturing footprint, with the data center segment being a key driver. The company's performance was driven by its ability to deliver differentiated, power-efficient, and high-performance solutions for high-growth markets, with management citing multiple secular megatrends reshaping the semiconductor industry. The stock reacted positively, moving up 14.9% on earnings day, driven by the headline beat and the announcement of the company's first-ever quarterly dividend, which underscored the company's confidence in its long-term growth prospects.
GFS.keyQuote: As we shared at today's Investor Day, GF is positioned at the center of multiple secular megatrends that are reshaping the semiconductor industry,

ASX.summary: ASE Technology reported Q1 2026 EPS of $0.20, beating estimates by 16.3%, driven by strong performance in the ATM segment, which saw a 30% year-over-year revenue increase. The company's packaging and testing businesses were key drivers of this growth, with revenue increases of 29% and 31%, respectively. The stock reacted positively to the earnings beat and forward guidance, which included projected 7-9% quarter-over-quarter revenue growth and a 20-100 basis point increase in gross margin.
ASX.keyQuote: Based on our current business outlook and exchange rate assumptions of 1 US dollar to 31.8 NT dollars, management projects overall performance for the second quarter of 2026 to be as follows

ASX.summary: ASE Technology reported Q4 2025 EPS of $0.21, beating estimates by 6.2%, driven by a 25% year-over-year increase in ATM revenue, with LEAP services reaching $1.6 billion, up from $0.6 billion in 2024. The company's strong performance was attributed to the AI supercycle, mainstream business recovery, and leadership in manufacturing, with management citing the Taiwan cluster's efficiency in infrastructure development and resource planning as a key driver. The stock reacted positively, moving up 6.7% on earnings day, likely due to the headline beat and forward guidance, including expectations for LEAP services to double to $3.2 billion in 2026.
ASX.keyQuote: For ATM business, LEAP services to double from US$1.6 billion to US$3.2 billion, with roughly 75% from packaging and 25% from testing.

DELL.summary: Dell's Q2 2026 earnings report is not provided, instead, the earnings report for SAIC is given, which shows an EPS beat of 64.0% with actual EPS of $4.86 vs estimate of $2.96, and revenue growth of 2% to $1.91 billion, driven by improved profitability across the contract portfolio and a $12 million gain from the sale of an investment. The specific business segments driving the beat are not explicitly stated, but the company mentions improved profitability across its contract portfolio. The stock reaction was a 32.8% increase, likely driven by the significant EPS beat and raised guidance for adjusted EBITDA, adjusted EBITDA margin, and adjusted diluted EPS.
DELL.keyQuote: I am proud of our team's performance this quarter, delivering record margin and modest organic growth, these results reflect our focus on execution and our commitment to our financial targets.

DELL.summary: Dell's Q1 2026 earnings report showed an EPS beat of 10.7% with actual EPS of $3.89 vs $3.51 estimate, however the provided text does not specify which business segments drove the beat, the performance was likely driven by specific product lines or customers that management cited, although the text does not provide this information. Investors were likely focused on the company's ability to meet expectations, and the reality appears to have matched or exceeded these expectations. The stock reaction was a 21.9% increase, likely driven by the headline beat and potentially other factors such as forward guidance or segment surprises, although the text does not provide specific information on this.

ANET.summary: Arista Networks reported Q1 2026 EPS of $0.87, beating estimates by 7.7%, driven by 35.1% year-over-year revenue growth to $2.709 billion, with the data center segment being a key driver. The company's performance was driven by its mission-critical confluence of secure client-to-campus-to-cloud and AI networking, with the introduction of XPO high-density liquid-cooled pluggable optics and the universal AI spine powered by the 7800. The stock reacted negatively, falling 13.6% on earnings day, likely due to the forward guidance of $2.8 billion in revenue and non-GAAP operating margin of 46-47%, which may have been perceived as conservative despite beating estimates.
ANET.keyQuote: We are uniquely positioned to deliver the mission-critical confluence of secure client-to-campus-to-cloud and AI networking.

ANET.summary: Arista Networks reported Q4 EPS of $0.82, beating estimates by 8.2%, driven by a 28.9% year-over-year increase in revenue to $2.488 billion, with strength in AI networking and campus expansion. The company's performance was driven by its Arista 2.0 momentum, including the shipment of 150 million cumulative ports, and the introduction of new products such as the R4 series platforms for AI, data center, and routed backbone deployment. The stock reacted positively, moving up 8.7% on earnings day, driven by the headline beat and the company's forward guidance, including revenue expectations of approximately $2.6 billion for the first quarter of 2026.
ANET.keyQuote: Our Q4 results underscore the strong operating leverage inherent in our model, providing a powerful exit rate as we head into 2026,

COHR.summary: Coherent (COHR) reported Q1 2026 EPS of $1.41, beating estimates by 1.1%, driven by higher investment income and a lower expense ratio in its P&C segments, which generated net written premium growth of 1% in the quarter. The company's P&C underlying combined ratio was 94.5%, compared with 92.1% in the prior year quarter, with a P&C underlying loss ratio of 64.1% and an expense ratio of 29.9%. The stock reacted positively, moving up 13.3% on the earnings day, likely due to the headline beat and the company's disciplined execution across its portfolio, as evidenced by its renewal premium change of +3% and written rate of +2%.

Coherent (COHR) reported Q4 EPS of $1.29, beating estimates by 6.8%, driven by strong performance in its Property & Casualty (P&C) segment, which generated net written premium growth of 2% and a combined ratio of 93.8%. The company's P&C core income of $449 million was driven by higher net investment income and lower underlying underwriting results, partially offset by higher catastrophe losses. The stock reacted negatively, falling 9.6% on the earnings day, likely due to the miss in the Life & Group segment and the decrease in net investment income from limited partnerships and common stock.

LITE.summary: Lumentum reported Q3 FY2026 EPS of $2.37, beating estimates of $2.27, with net revenue of $808.4 million, driven by a 90% year-over-year increase. The company's strong results were attributed to margin expansion, driven by operational rigor, pricing discipline, and product mix, with strength in laser chips and 'scale-across' components. Lumentum provided guidance for Q4 FY2026, expecting net revenue of $960-1.01 billion and non-GAAP diluted EPS of $2.85-3.05, which likely contributed to the 16.5% stock price increase on the earnings day.
LITE.keyQuote: Lumentum delivered an exceptional third quarter, with revenue growing 90% year over year to a record $808 million. While our top line growth continues to garner headlines, the more impressive part of our recent performance has been our margin expansion.

LITE.summary: Lumentum reported Q4 2025 earnings with an EPS of $1.67, beating the estimated $1.41, driven by the company's strong position in optical and photonic technologies that power AI, cloud computing, and next-generation communications. The main narrative emphasized by management was the strategic partnership with NVIDIA to develop state-of-the-art optics technology, which will accelerate innovation in advanced optics technologies and enable next-generation AI infrastructure and systems designs. The stock moved up 11.7% on the earnings day, likely due to the positive earnings surprise and the promising outlook for the company's future growth and collaboration with NVIDIA.
LITE.keyQuote: AI has reinvented computing and is driving the largest computing infrastructure buildout in history,

FN.summary: Fabrinet reported Q3 FY2026 EPS of $3.72, beating estimates of $3.56, with revenue of $1,214.3 million, driven by strong growth from numerous ongoing and ramping programs, particularly in the datacom market. The company's exceptionally strong quarter was supported by its advanced optical packaging and precision optical, electro-mechanical, and electronic manufacturing services. Fabrinet issued guidance for Q4 FY2026, expecting revenue of $1.25-1.29 billion and non-GAAP EPS of $3.72-3.87, and the stock moved up 12.7% on the earnings day, likely due to the strong beat and positive outlook, with the CEO noting that new customer agreements will further strengthen the company's growth trajectory, as stated by CEO Seamus Grady, who said,
FN.keyQuote: Our third quarter was exceptionally strong, delivering record financial results that exceeded our guidance ranges. Growth was supported by numerous ongoing and ramping programs that continue to provide meaningful tailwinds. We expect several new customer agreements, particularly in the datacom market, to further strengthen our growth trajectory as we move into the fourth quarter and beyond.

FN.summary: Fabrinet reported Q2 FY2026 EPS of $3.36, beating estimates of $3.25, with revenue of $1,132.9 million, driven by strong performance across multiple large strategic programs and excellent execution, which generated record earnings per share. The company's CEO, Seamus Grady, emphasized that the same drivers that produced these results will extend into the third quarter, reflecting the company's confidence in its strong guidance. Fabrinet issued guidance for Q3 FY2026, expecting revenue of $1.15-1.20 billion and non-GAAP EPS of $3.45-3.60, which likely contributed to the stock's 13.8% increase on earnings day.
FN.keyQuote: We delivered an exceptional second quarter with record revenue and earnings that significantly exceeded our guidance ranges.

AAPL.summary: Apple reported Q2 2026 earnings with EPS of $2.01, beating estimates of $1.94, and revenue of $111.2 billion, up 17% year over year. The strong results were driven by record revenue from iPhone sales, fueled by demand for the iPhone 17 lineup, as well as an all-time high in Services revenue. The stock moved up 3.2% on the earnings day, likely due to the company's beat on earnings and revenue, as well as its announcement of a 4% increase in dividend and a new $100 billion share repurchase program.
AAPL.keyQuote: Today Apple is proud to report our best March quarter ever, with revenue of $111.2 billion and double-digit growth across every geographic segment,

AAPL.summary: Apple reported Q1 2026 earnings with a diluted EPS of $2.84, beating estimates of $2.67, on revenue of $143.8 billion, up 16% year over year. The company's record-breaking quarter was driven by unprecedented demand for iPhones and an all-time revenue record for Services, with CEO Tim Cook noting the strength across every geographic segment. Despite the strong earnings beat, the stock moved -5.0% on earnings day, potentially due to high expectations and a lack of explicit guidance for future quarters, although the company did announce a cash dividend of $0.26 per share and reported nearly $54 billion in operating cash flow.
AAPL.keyQuote: Today, Apple is proud to report a remarkable, record-breaking quarter, with revenue of $143.8 billion, up 16 percent from a year ago and well above our expectations,

AMZN.summary: Amazon reported Q1 2026 earnings with EPS of $2.78, beating estimates of $1.65, and net sales increasing 17% to $181.5 billion. The company's strong growth was driven by significant increases in AWS sales, which rose 28% year-over-year, as well as growth in its chips business, advertising, and stores. The stock moved up 5.6% on the earnings day, likely due to the company's impressive beat and CEO Andy Jassy's optimistic outlook on the company's position to lead in major inflections, including AI and cloud computing.
AMZN.keyQuote: We’re making customers’ lives easier and better every day across all our businesses, and their response is driving significant growth

AMZN.summary: Amazon reported Q4 2025 earnings with an EPS of $1.95, missing the estimated $1.96, and net sales increased 14% to $213.4 billion. The company's growth was driven by strong demand for its existing offerings, including a 24% increase in AWS sales and a 22% increase in Advertising sales, with management emphasizing the importance of innovation and investment in areas like AI, chips, and robotics. The stock moved -5.6% on earnings day, likely due to the slight earnings miss, despite management's optimistic outlook and plans to invest $200 billion in capital expenditures in 2026, anticipating strong long-term returns.
AMZN.keyQuote: AWS growing 24% (our fastest growth in 13 quarters), Advertising growing 22%, Stores growing briskly across North America and International, our chips business growing triple digit percentages year-over-year—this growth is happening because we’re continuing to innovate at a rapid rate, and identify and knock down customer problems,

MSFT.summary: Microsoft reported Q1 2026 earnings with EPS of $4.27, beating estimates of $4.06, and revenue of $82.9 billion, up 18% year-over-year. The company's strong results were driven by its cloud and AI businesses, with Microsoft Cloud revenue increasing 29% and AI revenue surpassing an annual run rate of $37 billion, up 123% year-over-year. Microsoft will provide forward-looking guidance on its earnings conference call, and the stock moved up 5.4% on the earnings day, likely due to the company's strong execution and growing demand for its cloud services.
MSFT.keyQuote: We are focused on delivering cloud and AI infrastructure and solutions that empower every business to eval-max their outcomes in the agentic computing era,

MSFT.summary: Microsoft reported Q2 earnings with EPS of $4.14, beating estimates of $3.92, on revenue of $81.3 billion, which increased 17% year-over-year. The company's strong results were driven by its cloud and AI businesses, with Microsoft Cloud revenue crossing $50 billion and Azure and other cloud services revenue increasing 39%. Despite the strong earnings beat, the stock moved -10.0% on earnings day, and management will provide forward-looking guidance on its earnings conference call and webcast, which may have contributed to the stock's movement as investors await more information on the company's outlook.
MSFT.keyQuote: We are only at the beginning phases of AI diffusion and already Microsoft has built an AI business that is larger than some of our biggest franchises,

ORCL.summary: Oracle reported Q4 earnings per share of $2.11, beating estimates of $1.96, with record total revenues of $19.2 billion, up 21% year-over-year. The company's strong results were driven by broad-based demand for its cloud technology and applications suites, particularly in Cloud Infrastructure, which saw 93% growth. Oracle provided strong guidance for Q1 FY 2027, with total revenues expected to grow 27-29% and total cloud revenue expected to grow 57-63%, but the stock still moved -9.6% on earnings day, potentially due to high expectations and the significant capital investments required to support the growth of its Cloud Infrastructure business.

Oracle reported Q1 2026 earnings with an EPS of $1.79, beating estimates of $1.69, driven by strong demand for cloud infrastructure, AI training and inferencing, multicloud database, and cloud applications, which has given the company the opportunity to build and scale its cloud infrastructure rapidly. The company's strong performance was attributed to its ability to meet the growing demand for its services, with organic total revenue and non-GAAP earnings per share exceeding 20% growth. The stock moved up 12.7% on earnings day, likely due to the company's impressive growth momentum and the appointment of a new CFO, Hilary Maxson, who is expected to help the company continue to invest with discipline and translate its momentum into durable, long-term value for customers and shareholders.
ORCL.keyQuote: Oracle has built extraordinary momentum at the intersection of cloud, AI, and industry applications. I’m excited to join at this pivotal moment, and I look forward to partnering with Clay, Mike, and the broader leadership team to continue to invest with discipline and to translate this momentum into durable, long-term value for customers and shareholders.
